units.text: Believe me, there is no such thing in me.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
What made me love thee? let that persuade thee
there's something extraordinary in thee. Come, I
cannot cog and say thou art this and that, like a
many of these lisping hawthorn-buds, that come like
women in men's apparel, and smell like Bucklersbury
in simple time; I cannot: but I love thee; none
but thee; and thou deservest it.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: Do not betray me, sir. I fear you love Mistress Page.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Thou mightst as well say I love to walk by the
Counter-gate, which is as hateful to me as the reek
of a lime-kiln.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
Well, heaven knows how I love you; and you shall one
day find it.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Keep in that mind; I'll deserve it.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
Nay, I must tell you, so you do; or else I could not
be in that mind.

units.speaker: ROBIN
units.text:
[Within] Mistress Ford, Mistress Ford! here's
Mistress Page at the door, sweating and blowing and
looking wildly, and would needs speak with you
presently.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
She shall not see me: I will ensconce me behind the
arras.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: Pray you, do so: she's a very tattling woman.
FALSTAFF hides himself
Re-enter MISTRESS PAGE and ROBIN

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: What's the matter? how now!

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
O Mistress Ford, what have you done? You're shamed,
you're overthrown, you're undone for ever!

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: What's the matter, good Mistress Page?

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
O well-a-day, Mistress Ford! having an honest man
to your husband, to give him such cause of suspicion!

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: What cause of suspicion?

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
What cause of suspicion! Out pon you! how am I
mistook in you!

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: Why, alas, what's the matter?

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Your husband's coming hither, woman, with all the
officers in Windsor, to search for a gentleman that
he says is here now in the house by your consent, to
take an ill advantage of his assence: you are undone.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: 'Tis not so, I hope.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Pray heaven it be not so, that you have such a man
here! but 'tis most certain your husband's coming,
with half Windsor at his heels, to search for such a
one. I come before to tell you. If you know
yourself clear, why, I am glad of it; but if you
have a friend here convey, convey him out. Be not
amazed; call all your senses to you; defend your
reputation, or bid farewell to your good life for ever.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
What shall I do? There is a gentleman my dear
friend; and I fear not mine own shame so much as his
peril: I had rather than a thousand pound he were
out of the house.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
For shame! never stand 'you had rather' and 'you
had rather:' your husband's here at hand, bethink
you of some conveyance: in the house you cannot
hide him. O, how have you deceived me! Look, here
is a basket: if he be of any reasonable stature, he
may creep in here; and throw foul linen upon him, as
if it were going to bucking: or—it is whiting-time
—send him by your two men to Datchet-mead.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: He's too big to go in there. What shall I do?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
[Coming forward] Let me see't, let me see't, O, let
me see't! I'll in, I'll in. Follow your friend's
counsel. I'll in.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
What, Sir John Falstaff! Are these your letters,
knight?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
I love thee. Help me away. Let me creep in here.
I'll never—

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Help to cover your master, boy. Call your men,
Mistress Ford. You dissembling knight!

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: What, John! Robert! John!
Exit ROBIN
Re-enter Servants

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
Go take up these clothes here quickly. Where's the
cowl-staff? look, how you drumble! Carry them to
the laundress in Datchet-meat; quickly, come.

units.speaker: FORD
SERVANT
units.text: To the laundress, forsooth.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
Why, what have you to do whither they bear it? You
were best meddle with buck-washing.

units.speaker: FORD

units.text: Exeunt Servants with the basket

units.speaker: FORD
units.text:
Gentlemen, I have dreamed to-night; I'll tell you my
dream. Here, here, here be my keys: ascend my
chambers; search, seek, find out: I'll warrant
we'll unkennel the fox. Let me stop this way first.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: So, now uncape.

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
Good Master Ford, be contented: you wrong yourself too
much.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text:
True, Master Page. Up, gentlemen: you shall see
sport anon: follow me, gentlemen.
Exit

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text: This is fery fantastical humours and jealousies.

units.speaker: DOCTOR CAIUS
units.text:
By gar, 'tis no the fashion of France; it is not
jealous in France.

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
Nay, follow him, gentlemen; see the issue of his
search.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text: Is there not a double excellency in this?

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
I know not which pleases me better, that my husband
is deceived, or Sir John.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
What a taking was he in when your husband asked who
was in the basket!

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
I am half afraid he will have need of washing; so
throwing him into the water will do him a benefit.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Hang him, dishonest rascal! I would all of the same
strain were in the same distress.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
I think my husband hath some special suspicion of
Falstaff's being here; for I never saw him so gross
in his jealousy till now.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
I will lay a plot to try that; and we will yet have
more tricks with Falstaff: his dissolute disease will
scarce obey this medicine.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
Shall we send that foolish carrion, Mistress
Quickly, to him, and excuse his throwing into the
water; and give him another hope, to betray him to
another punishment?

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
We will do it: let him be sent for to-morrow,
eight o'clock, to have amends.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text:
I cannot find him: may be the knave bragged of that
he could not compass.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text: [Aside to MISTRESS FORD] Heard you that?

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: You use me well, Master Ford, do you?

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: Ay, I do so.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: Heaven make you better than your thoughts!

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: Amen!

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text: You do yourself mighty wrong, Master Ford.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: Ay, ay; I must bear it.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text:
If there be any pody in the house, and in the
chambers, and in the coffers, and in the presses,
heaven forgive my sins at the day of judgment!

units.speaker: DOCTOR CAIUS
units.text: By gar, nor I too: there is no bodies.

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
Fie, fie, Master Ford! are you not ashamed? What
spirit, what devil suggests this imagination? I
would not ha' your distemper in this kind for the
wealth of Windsor Castle.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: 'Tis my fault, Master Page: I suffer for it.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text:
You suffer for a pad conscience: your wife is as
honest a 'omans as I will desires among five
thousand, and five hundred too.

units.speaker: DOCTOR CAIUS
units.text: By gar, I see 'tis an honest woman.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text:
Well, I promised you a dinner. Come, come, walk in
the Park: I pray you, pardon me; I will hereafter
make known to you why I have done this. Come,
wife; come, Mistress Page. I pray you, pardon me;
pray heartily, pardon me.

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
Let's go in, gentlemen; but, trust me, we'll mock
him. I do invite you to-morrow morning to my house
to breakfast: after, we'll a-birding together; I
have a fine hawk for the bush. Shall it be so?

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: Any thing.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text: If there is one, I shall make two in the company.

units.speaker: DOCTOR CAIUS
units.text: If dere be one or two, I shall make-a the turd.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: Pray you, go, Master Page.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text:
I pray you now, remembrance tomorrow on the lousy
knave, mine host.

units.speaker: DOCTOR CAIUS
units.text: Dat is good; by gar, with all my heart!

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text: A lousy knave, to have his gibes and his mockeries!
Exeunt
Enter FENTON and ANNE PAGE

units.speaker: FENTON
units.text:
I see I cannot get thy father's love;
Therefore no more turn me to him, sweet Nan.

units.speaker: ANNE PAGE
units.text: Alas, how then?

units.speaker: FENTON
units.text:
Why, thou must be thyself.
He doth object I am too great of birth—,
And that, my state being gall'd with my expense,
I seek to heal it only by his wealth:
Besides these, other bars he lays before me,
My riots past, my wild societies;
And tells me 'tis a thing impossible
I should love thee but as a property.

units.speaker: ANNE PAGE
units.text: May be he tells you true.

units.speaker: FENTON
units.text:
No, heaven so speed me in my time to come!
Albeit I will confess thy father's wealth
Was the first motive that I woo'd thee, Anne:
Yet, wooing thee, I found thee of more value
Than stamps in gold or sums in sealed bags;
And 'tis the very riches of thyself
That now I aim at.

units.speaker: ANNE PAGE
units.text:
Gentle Master Fenton,
Yet seek my father's love; still seek it, sir:
If opportunity and humblest suit
Cannot attain it, why, then,—hark you hither!
Enter SHALLOW, SLENDER, and MISTRESS QUICKLY

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text:
Break their talk, Mistress Quickly: my kinsman shall
speak for himself.

units.speaker: SLENDER
units.text:
I'll make a shaft or a bolt on't: 'slid, 'tis but
venturing.

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text: Be not dismayed.

units.speaker: SLENDER
units.text:
No, she shall not dismay me: I care not for that,
but that I am afeard.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: Hark ye; Master Slender would speak a word with you.

units.speaker: ANNE PAGE
units.text: I come to him.
Aside

units.speaker: ANNE PAGE
units.text:
This is my father's choice.
O, what a world of vile ill-favor'd faults
Looks handsome in three hundred pounds a-year!

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
And how does good Master Fenton? Pray you, a word with
you.

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text: She's coming; to her, coz. O boy, thou hadst a father!

units.speaker: SLENDER
units.text:
I had a father, Mistress Anne; my uncle can tell you
good jests of him. Pray you, uncle, tell Mistress
Anne the jest, how my father stole two geese out of
a pen, good uncle.

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text: Mistress Anne, my cousin loves you.

units.speaker: SLENDER
units.text:
Ay, that I do; as well as I love any woman in
Gloucestershire.

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text: He will maintain you like a gentlewoman.

units.speaker: SLENDER
units.text:
Ay, that I will, come cut and long-tail, under the
degree of a squire.

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text: He will make you a hundred and fifty pounds jointure.

units.speaker: ANNE PAGE
units.text: Good Master Shallow, let him woo for himself.

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text:
Marry, I thank you for it; I thank you for that good
comfort. She calls you, coz: I'll leave you.

units.speaker: ANNE PAGE
units.text: Now, Master Slender,—

units.speaker: SLENDER
units.text: Now, good Mistress Anne,—

units.speaker: ANNE PAGE
units.text: What is your will?

units.speaker: SLENDER
units.text:
My will! 'od's heartlings, that's a pretty jest
indeed! I ne'er made my will yet, I thank heaven; I
am not such a sickly creature, I give heaven praise.

units.speaker: ANNE PAGE
units.text: I mean, Master Slender, what would you with me?

units.speaker: SLENDER
units.text:
Truly, for mine own part, I would little or nothing
with you. Your father and my uncle hath made
motions: if it be my luck, so; if not, happy man be
his dole! They can tell you how things go better
than I can: you may ask your father; here he comes.
Enter PAGE and MISTRESS PAGE

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
Now, Master Slender: love him, daughter Anne.
Why, how now! what does Master Fenton here?
You wrong me, sir, thus still to haunt my house:
I told you, sir, my daughter is disposed of.

units.speaker: FENTON
units.text: Nay, Master Page, be not impatient.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text: Good Master Fenton, come not to my child.

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text: She is no match for you.

units.speaker: FENTON
units.text: Sir, will you hear me?

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
No, good Master Fenton.
Come, Master Shallow; come, son Slender, in.
Knowing my mind, you wrong me, Master Fenton.
Exeunt PAGE, SHALLOW, and SLENDER

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: Speak to Mistress Page.

units.speaker: FENTON
units.text:
Good Mistress Page, for that I love your daughter
In such a righteous fashion as I do,
Perforce, against all cheques, rebukes and manners,
I must advance the colours of my love
And not retire: let me have your good will.

units.speaker: ANNE PAGE
units.text: Good mother, do not marry me to yond fool.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text: I mean it not; I seek you a better husband.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: That's my master, master doctor.

units.speaker: ANNE PAGE
units.text:
Alas, I had rather be set quick i' the earth
And bowl'd to death with turnips!

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Come, trouble not yourself. Good Master Fenton,
I will not be your friend nor enemy:
My daughter will I question how she loves you,
And as I find her, so am I affected.
Till then farewell, sir: she must needs go in;
Her father will be angry.

units.speaker: FENTON
units.text: Farewell, gentle mistress: farewell, Nan.
Exeunt MISTRESS PAGE and ANNE PAGE

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
This is my doing, now: 'Nay,' said I, 'will you cast
away your child on a fool, and a physician? Look on
Master Fenton:' this is my doing.

units.speaker: FENTON
units.text:
I thank thee; and I pray thee, once to-night
Give my sweet Nan this ring: there's for thy pains.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: Now heaven send thee good fortune!
Exit FENTON

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
A kind heart he hath: a woman would run through
fire and water for such a kind heart. But yet I
would my master had Mistress Anne; or I would
Master Slender had her; or, in sooth, I would Master
Fenton had her; I will do what I can for them all
three; for so I have promised, and I'll be as good
as my word; but speciously for Master Fenton. Well,
I must of another errand to Sir John Falstaff from
my two mistresses: what a beast am I to slack it!
Exit
Enter FALSTAFF and BARDOLPH

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Bardolph, I say,—

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text: Here, sir.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Go fetch me a quart of sack; put a toast in't.
Exit BARDOLPH

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Have I lived to be carried in a basket, like a
barrow of butcher's offal, and to be thrown in the
Thames? Well, if I be served such another trick,
I'll have my brains ta'en out and buttered, and give
them to a dog for a new-year's gift. The rogues
slighted me into the river with as little remorse as
they would have drowned a blind bitch's puppies,
fifteen i' the litter: and you may know by my size
that I have a kind of alacrity in sinking; if the
bottom were as deep as hell, I should down. I had
been drowned, but that the shore was shelvy and
shallow,—a death that I abhor; for the water swells
a man; and what a thing should I have been when I
had been swelled! I should have been a mountain of
mummy.
Re-enter BARDOLPH with sack

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text: Here's Mistress Quickly, sir, to speak with you.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
L et me pour in some sack to the Thames water; for my
belly's as cold as if I had swallowed snowballs for
pills to cool the reins. Call her in.

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text: Come in, woman!
Enter MISTRESS QUICKLY

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
By your leave; I cry you mercy: give your worship
good morrow.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Take away these chalices. Go brew me a pottle of
sack finely.

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text: With eggs, sir?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Simple of itself; I'll no pullet-sperm in my brewage.
Exit BARDOLPH

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: How now!

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: Marry, sir, I come to your worship from Mistress Ford.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Mistress Ford! I have had ford enough; I was thrown
into the ford; I have my belly full of ford.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Alas the day! good heart, that was not her fault:
she does so take on with her men; they mistook their
erection.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: So did I mine, to build upon a foolish woman's promise.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Well, she laments, sir, for it, that it would yearn
your heart to see it. Her husband goes this morning
a-birding; she desires you once more to come to her
between eight and nine: I must carry her word
quickly: she'll make you amends, I warrant you.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Well, I will visit her: tell her so; and bid her
think what a man is: let her consider his frailty,
and then judge of my merit.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: I will tell her.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Do so. Between nine and ten, sayest thou?

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: Eight and nine, sir.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Well, be gone: I will not miss her.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: Peace be with you, sir.
Exit

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
I marvel I hear not of Master Brook; he sent me word
to stay within: I like his money well. O, here he
comes.
Enter FORD

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: Bless you, sir!

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Now, master Brook, you come to know what hath passed
between me and Ford's wife?

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: That, indeed, Sir John, is my business.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Master Brook, I will not lie to you: I was at her
house the hour she appointed me.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: And sped you, sir?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Very ill-favoredly, Master Brook.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: How so, sir? Did she change her determination?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
No, Master Brook; but the peaking Cornuto her
husband, Master Brook, dwelling in a continual
'larum of jealousy, comes me in the instant of our
encounter, after we had embraced, kissed, protested,
and, as it were, spoke the prologue of our comedy;
and at his heels a rabble of his companions, thither
provoked and instigated by his distemper, and,
forsooth, to search his house for his wife's love.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: What, while you were there?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: While I was there.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: And did he search for you, and could not find you?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
You shall hear. As good luck would have it, comes
in one Mistress Page; gives intelligence of Ford's
approach; and, in her invention and Ford's wife's
distraction, they conveyed me into a buck-basket.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: A buck-basket!

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
By the Lord, a buck-basket! rammed me in with foul
shirts and smocks, socks, foul stockings, greasy
napkins; that, Master Brook, there was the rankest
compound of villanous smell that ever offended nostril.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: And how long lay you there?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Nay, you shall hear, Master Brook, what I have
suffered to bring this woman to evil for your good.
Being thus crammed in the basket, a couple of Ford's
knaves, his hinds, were called forth by their
mistress to carry me in the name of foul clothes to
Datchet-lane: they took me on their shoulders; met
the jealous knave their master in the door, who
asked them once or twice what they had in their
basket: I quaked for fear, lest the lunatic knave
would have searched it; but fate, ordaining he
should be a cuckold, held his hand. Well: on went he
for a search, and away went I for foul clothes. But
mark the sequel, Master Brook: I suffered the pangs
of three several deaths; first, an intolerable
fright, to be detected with a jealous rotten
bell-wether; next, to be compassed, like a good
bilbo, in the circumference of a peck, hilt to
point, heel to head; and then, to be stopped in,
like a strong distillation, with stinking clothes
that fretted in their own grease: think of that,—a
man of my kidney,—think of that,—that am as subject
to heat as butter; a man of continual dissolution
and thaw: it was a miracle to scape suffocation.
And in the height of this bath, when I was more than
half stewed in grease, like a Dutch dish, to be
thrown into the Thames, and cooled, glowing hot,
in that surge, like a horse-shoe; think of
that,—hissing hot,—think of that, Master Brook.

units.speaker: FORD
FALSTAFF
units.text:
Master Brook, I will be thrown into Etna, as I have
been into Thames, ere I will leave her thus. Her
husband is this morning gone a-birding: I have
received from her another embassy of meeting; 'twixt
eight and nine is the hour, Master Brook.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: 'Tis past eight already, sir.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Is it? I will then address me to my appointment.
Come to me at your convenient leisure, and you shall
know how I speed; and the conclusion shall be
crowned with your enjoying her. Adieu. You shall
have her, Master Brook; Master Brook, you shall
cuckold Ford.
Exit

units.speaker: FORD
units.text:
Hum! ha! is this a vision? is this a dream? do I
sleep? Master Ford awake! awake, Master Ford!
there's a hole made in your best coat, Master Ford.
This 'tis to be married! this 'tis to have linen
and buck-baskets! Well, I will proclaim myself
what I am: I will now take the lecher; he is at my
house; he cannot 'scape me; 'tis impossible he
should; he cannot creep into a halfpenny purse,
nor into a pepper-box: but, lest the devil that
guides him should aid him, I will search
impossible places. Though what I am I cannot avoid,
yet to be what I would not shall not make me tame:
if I have horns to make one mad, let the proverb go
with me: I'll be horn-mad.
Exit
Enter MISTRESS PAGE, MISTRESS QUICKLY, and WILLIAM PAGE

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text: Is he at Master Ford's already, think'st thou?

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Sure he is by this, or will be presently: but,
truly, he is very courageous mad about his throwing
into the water. Mistress Ford desires you to come
suddenly.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
MISTRESS PAGE
units.text: How now, Sir Hugh! no school to-day?

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text: No; Master Slender is let the boys leave to play.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: Blessing of his heart!

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Sir Hugh, my husband says my son profits nothing in
the world at his book. I pray you, ask him some
questions in his accidence.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text: Come hither, William; hold up your head; come.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Come on, sirrah; hold up your head; answer your
master, be not afraid.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text: William, how many numbers is in nouns?

units.speaker: WILLIAM PAGE
units.text: Two.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Truly, I thought there had been one number more,
because they say, ''Od's nouns.'

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text: Peace your tattlings! What is 'fair,' William?

units.speaker: WILLIAM PAGE
units.text: Pulcher.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: Polecats! there are fairer things than polecats, sure.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text:
You are a very simplicity 'oman: I pray you peace.
What is 'lapis,' William?

units.speaker: WILLIAM PAGE
units.text: A stone.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text: And what is 'a stone,' William?

units.speaker: WILLIAM PAGE
units.text: A pebble.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text: No, it is 'lapis:' I pray you, remember in your prain.

units.speaker: WILLIAM PAGE
units.text: Lapis.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text:
That is a good William. What is he, William, that
does lend articles?

units.speaker: WILLIAM PAGE
units.text:
Articles are borrowed of the pronoun, and be thus
declined, Singulariter, nominativo, hic, haec, hoc.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text:
Nominativo, hig, hag, hog; pray you, mark:
genitivo, hujus. Well, what is your accusative case?

units.speaker: WILLIAM PAGE
units.text: Accusativo, hinc.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text:
I pray you, have your remembrance, child,
accusative, hung, hang, hog.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: 'Hang-hog' is Latin for bacon, I warrant you.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text:
Leave your prabbles, 'oman. What is the focative
case, William?

units.speaker: WILLIAM PAGE
units.text: O,—vocativo, O.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text: Remember, William; focative is caret.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: And that's a good root.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text: 'Oman, forbear.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text: Peace!

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text: What is your genitive case plural, William?

units.speaker: WILLIAM PAGE
units.text: Genitive case!

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text: Ay.

units.speaker: WILLIAM PAGE
units.text: Genitive,—horum, harum, horum.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Vengeance of Jenny's case! fie on her! never name
her, child, if she be a whore.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text: For shame, 'oman.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
You do ill to teach the child such words: he
teaches him to hick and to hack, which they'll do
fast enough of themselves, and to call 'horum:' fie
upon you!

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text:
'Oman, art thou lunatics? hast thou no
understandings for thy cases and the numbers of the
genders? Thou art as foolish Christian creatures as
I would desires.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text: Prithee, hold thy peace.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text:
Show me now, William, some declensions of your
pronouns.

units.speaker: WILLIAM PAGE
units.text: Forsooth, I have forgot.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text:
It is qui, quae, quod: if you forget your 'quies,'
your 'quaes,' and your 'quods,' you must be
preeches. Go your ways, and play; go.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text: He is a better scholar than I thought he was.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text: He is a good sprag memory. Farewell, Mistress Page.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text: Adieu, good Sir Hugh.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text: Get you home, boy. Come, we stay too long.
Exeunt
Enter FALSTAFF and MISTRESS FORD

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Mistress Ford, your sorrow hath eaten up my
sufferance. I see you are obsequious in your love,
and I profess requital to a hair's breadth; not
only, Mistress Ford, in the simple
office of love, but in all the accoutrement,
complement and ceremony of it. But are you
sure of your husband now?

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: He's a-birding, sweet Sir John.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text: [Within] What, ho, gossip Ford! what, ho!

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: Step into the chamber, Sir John.
Exit FALSTAFF
Enter MISTRESS PAGE

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text: How now, sweetheart! who's at home besides yourself?

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: Why, none but mine own people.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text: Indeed!

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
No, certainly.
[Aside to her] Speak louder.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text: Truly, I am so glad you have nobody here.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: Why?

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Why, woman, your husband is in his old lunes again:
he so takes on yonder with my husband; so rails
against all married mankind; so curses all Eve's
daughters, of what complexion soever; and so buffets
himself on the forehead, crying, 'Peer out, peer
out!' that any madness I ever yet beheld seemed but
tameness, civility and patience, to this his
distemper he is in now: I am glad the fat knight is not
here.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: Why, does he talk of him?

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Of none but him; and swears he was carried out, the
last time he searched for him, in a basket; protests
to my husband he is now here, and hath drawn him and
the rest of their company from their sport, to make
another experiment of his suspicion: but I am glad
the knight is not here; now he shall see his own
foolery.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: How near is he, Mistress Page?

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text: Hard by; at street end; he will be here anon.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: I am undone! The knight is here.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Why then you are utterly shamed, and he's but a dead
man. What a woman are you!—Away with him, away
with him! better shame than murder.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
Which way should be go? how should I bestow him?
Shall I put him into the basket again?

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
Which way should be go? how should I bestow him?
Shall I put him into the basket again?
Re-enter FALSTAFF

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
No, I'll come no more i' the basket. May I not go
out ere he come?

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Alas, three of Master Ford's brothers watch the door
with pistols, that none shall issue out; otherwise
you might slip away ere he came. But what make you
here?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: What shall I do? I'll creep up into the chimney.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
There they always use to discharge their
birding-pieces. Creep into the kiln-hole.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Where is it?

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
He will seek there, on my word. Neither press,
coffer, chest, trunk, well, vault, but he hath an
abstract for the remembrance of such places, and
goes to them by his note: there is no hiding you in the
house.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: I'll go out then.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
If you go out in your own semblance, you die, Sir
John. Unless you go out disguised—

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: How might we disguise him?

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Alas the day, I know not! There is no woman's gown
big enough for him otherwise he might put on a hat,
a muffler and a kerchief, and so escape.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Good hearts, devise something: any extremity rather
than a mischief.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
My maid's aunt, the fat woman of Brentford, has a
gown above.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
On my word, it will serve him; she's as big as he
is: and there's her thrummed hat and her muffler
too. Run up, Sir John.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
Go, go, sweet Sir John: Mistress Page and I will
look some linen for your head.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Quick, quick! we'll come dress you straight: put
on the gown the while.
Exit FALSTAFF

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
I would my husband would meet him in this shape: he
cannot abide the old woman of Brentford; he swears
she's a witch; forbade her my house and hath
threatened to beat her.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Heaven guide him to thy husband's cudgel, and the
devil guide his cudgel afterwards!

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: But is my husband coming?

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Ah, in good sadness, is he; and talks of the basket
too, howsoever he hath had intelligence.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
We'll try that; for I'll appoint my men to carry the
basket again, to meet him at the door with it, as
they did last time.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Nay, but he'll be here presently: let's go dress him
like the witch of Brentford.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
I'll first direct my men what they shall do with the
basket. Go up; I'll bring linen for him straight.
Exit

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Hang him, dishonest varlet! we cannot misuse him
enough.
We'll leave a proof, by that which we will do,
Wives may be merry, and yet honest too:
We do not act that often jest and laugh;
'Tis old, but true, Still swine eat all the draff.
Exit
Re-enter MISTRESS FORD with two Servants

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
Go, sirs, take the basket again on your shoulders:
your master is hard at door; if he bid you set it
down, obey him: quickly, dispatch.
Exit

units.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
units.text: Come, come, take it up.

units.speaker: SECOND SERVANT
units.text: Pray heaven it be not full of knight again.

units.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
units.text: I hope not; I had as lief bear so much lead.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text:
Ay, but if it prove true, Master Page, have you any
way then to unfool me again? Set down the basket,
villain! Somebody call my wife. Youth in a basket!
O you panderly rascals! there's a knot, a ging, a
pack, a conspiracy against me: now shall the devil
be shamed. What, wife, I say! Come, come forth!
Behold what honest clothes you send forth to bleaching!

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
Why, this passes, Master Ford; you are not to go
loose any longer; you must be pinioned.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text: Why, this is lunatics! this is mad as a mad dog!

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text: Indeed, Master Ford, this is not well, indeed.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: So say I too, sir.
Re-enter MISTRESS FORD

units.speaker: FORD
units.text:
Come hither, Mistress Ford; Mistress Ford the honest
woman, the modest wife, the virtuous creature, that
hath the jealous fool to her husband! I suspect
without cause, mistress, do I?

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
Heaven be my witness you do, if you suspect me in
any dishonesty.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text:
Well said, brazen-face! hold it out. Come forth,
sirrah!

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text: This passes!

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: Are you not ashamed? let the clothes alone.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: I shall find you anon.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text:
'Tis unreasonable! Will you take up your wife's
clothes? Come away.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: Empty the basket, I say!

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: Why, man, why?

units.speaker: FORD
units.text:
Master Page, as I am a man, there was one conveyed
out of my house yesterday in this basket: why may
not he be there again? In my house I am sure he is:
my intelligence is true; my jealousy is reasonable.
Pluck me out all the linen.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: If you find a man there, he shall die a flea's death.

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text: Here's no man.

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text:
By my fidelity, this is not well, Master Ford; this
wrongs you.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text:
Master Ford, you must pray, and not follow the
imaginations of your own heart: this is jealousies.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: Well, he's not here I seek for.

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text: No, nor nowhere else but in your brain.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text:
Help to search my house this one time. If I find
not what I seek, show no colour for my extremity; let
me for ever be your table-sport; let them say of
me, 'As jealous as Ford, that searched a hollow
walnut for his wife's leman.' Satisfy me once more;
once more search with me.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
What, ho, Mistress Page! come you and the old woman
down; my husband will come into the chamber.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: Old woman! what old woman's that?

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: Nay, it is my maid's aunt of Brentford.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text:
A witch, a quean, an old cozening quean! Have I not
forbid her my house? She comes of errands, does
she? We are simple men; we do not know what's
brought to pass under the profession of
fortune-telling. She works by charms, by spells,
by the figure, and such daubery as this is, beyond
our element we know nothing. Come down, you witch,
you hag, you; come down, I say!

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
Nay, good, sweet husband! Good gentlemen, let him
not strike the old woman.
Re-enter FALSTAFF in woman's clothes, and MISTRESS PAGE

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text: Come, Mother Prat; come, give me your hand.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: I'll prat her.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text:
Out of my door, you witch, you hag, you baggage, you
polecat, you runyon! out, out! I'll conjure you,
I'll fortune-tell you.
Exit FALSTAFF

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Are you not ashamed? I think you have killed the
poor woman.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: Nay, he will do it. 'Tis a goodly credit for you.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: Hang her, witch!

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
FORD
PAGE
units.text:
Let's obey his humour a little further: come,
gentlemen.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text: Trust me, he beat him most pitifully.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
Nay, by the mass, that he did not; he beat him most
unpitifully, methought.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
I'll have the cudgel hallowed and hung o'er the
altar; it hath done meritorious service.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
What think you? may we, with the warrant of
womanhood and the witness of a good conscience,
pursue him with any further revenge?

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
The spirit of wantonness is, sure, scared out of
him: if the devil have him not in fee-simple, with
fine and recovery, he will never, I think, in the
way of waste, attempt us again.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: Shall we tell our husbands how we have served him?

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Yes, by all means; if it be but to scrape the
figures out of your husband's brains. If they can
find in their hearts the poor unvirtuous fat knight
shall be any further afflicted, we two will still be
the ministers.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
I'll warrant they'll have him publicly shamed: and
methinks there would be no period to the jest,
should he not be publicly shamed.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Come, to the forge with it then; shape it: I would
not have things cool.
Exeunt
Enter Host and BARDOLPH

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text:
Sir, the Germans desire to have three of your
horses: the duke himself will be to-morrow at
court, and they are going to meet him.

units.speaker: HOST
units.text:
What duke should that be comes so secretly? I hear
not of him in the court. Let me speak with the
gentlemen: they speak English?

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text: Ay, sir; I'll call them to you.

units.speaker: HOST
units.text:
They shall have my horses; but I'll make them pay;
I'll sauce them: they have had my house a week at
command; I have turned away my other guests: they
must come off; I'll sauce them. Come.
Exeunt
Enter PAGE, FORD, MISTRESS PAGE, MISTRESS FORD, and SIR HUGH EVANS

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text:
'Tis one of the best discretions of a 'oman as ever
I did look upon.

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text: And did he send you both these letters at an instant?
Within a quarter of an hour.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text:
Pardon me, wife. Henceforth do what thou wilt;
I rather will suspect the sun with cold
Than thee with wantonness: now doth thy honour stand
In him that was of late an heretic,
As firm as faith.

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
'Tis well, 'tis well; no more:
Be not as extreme in submission
As in offence.
But let our plot go forward: let our wives
Yet once again, to make us public sport,
Appoint a meeting with this old fat fellow,
Where we may take him and disgrace him for it.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: There is no better way than that they spoke of.

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
How? to send him word they'll meet him in the park
at midnight? Fie, fie! he'll never come.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text:
You say he has been thrown in the rivers and has
been grievously peaten as an old 'oman: methinks
there should be terrors in him that he should not
come; methinks his flesh is punished, he shall have
no desires.

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text: So think I too.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
Devise but how you'll use him when he comes,
And let us two devise to bring him thither.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
There is an old tale goes that Herne the hunter,
Sometime a keeper here in Windsor forest,
Doth all the winter-time, at still midnight,
Walk round about an oak, with great ragg'd horns;
And there he blasts the tree and takes the cattle
And makes milch-kine yield blood and shakes a chain
In a most hideous and dreadful manner:
You have heard of such a spirit, and well you know
The superstitious idle-headed eld
Received and did deliver to our age
This tale of Herne the hunter for a truth.

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
Why, yet there want not many that do fear
In deep of night to walk by this Herne's oak:
But what of this?

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
Marry, this is our device;
That Falstaff at that oak shall meet with us.

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
Well, let it not be doubted but he'll come:
And in this shape when you have brought him thither,
What shall be done with him? what is your plot?

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
That likewise have we thought upon, and thus:
Nan Page my daughter and my little son
And three or four more of their growth we'll dress
Like urchins, ouphes and fairies, green and white,
With rounds of waxen tapers on their heads,
And rattles in their hands: upon a sudden,
As Falstaff, she and I, are newly met,
Let them from forth a sawpit rush at once
With some diffused song: upon their sight,
We two in great amazedness will fly:
Then let them all encircle him about
And, fairy-like, to-pinch the unclean knight,
And ask him why, that hour of fairy revel,
In their so sacred paths he dares to tread
In shape profane.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
And till he tell the truth,
Let the supposed fairies pinch him sound
And burn him with their tapers.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
The truth being known,
We'll all present ourselves, dis-horn the spirit,
And mock him home to Windsor.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text:
The children must
Be practised well to this, or they'll ne'er do't.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text:
I will teach the children their behaviors; and I
will be like a jack-an-apes also, to burn the
knight with my taber.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: That will be excellent. I'll go and buy them vizards.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
My Nan shall be the queen of all the fairies,
Finely attired in a robe of white.

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text: That silk will I go buy.

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
And in that time
Shall Master Slender steal my Nan away
And marry her at Eton. Go send to Falstaff straight.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text:
Nay I'll to him again in name of Brook
He'll tell me all his purpose: sure, he'll come.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Fear not you that. Go get us properties
And tricking for our fairies.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text:
Let us about it: it is admirable pleasures and fery
honest knaveries.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Go, Mistress Ford,
Send quickly to Sir John, to know his mind.
Exit MISTRESS FORD

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
I'll to the doctor: he hath my good will,
And none but he, to marry with Nan Page.
That Slender, though well landed, is an idiot;
And he my husband best of all affects.
The doctor is well money'd, and his friends
Potent at court: he, none but he, shall have her,
Though twenty thousand worthier come to crave her.
Exit
Enter Host and SIMPLE

units.speaker: HOST
units.text:
What wouldst thou have, boor? what: thick-skin?
speak, breathe, discuss; brief, short, quick, snap.

units.speaker: SIMPLE
units.text:
Marry, sir, I come to speak with Sir John Falstaff
from Master Slender.

units.speaker: HOST
units.text:
There's his chamber, his house, his castle, his
standing-bed and truckle-bed; 'tis painted about
with the story of the Prodigal, fresh and new. Go
knock and call; hell speak like an Anthropophaginian
unto thee: knock, I say.

units.speaker: SIMPLE
units.text:
There's an old woman, a fat woman, gone up into his
chamber: I'll be so bold as stay, sir, till she come
down; I come to speak with her, indeed.

units.speaker: HOST
units.text:
Ha! a fat woman! the knight may be robbed: I'll
call. Bully knight! bully Sir John! speak from
thy lungs military: art thou there? it is thine
host, thine Ephesian, calls.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: [Above] How now, mine host!

units.speaker: HOST
units.text:
Here's a Bohemian-Tartar tarries the coming down of
thy fat woman. Let her descend, bully, let her
descend; my chambers are honourable: fie! privacy?
fie!
Enter FALSTAFF

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
There was, mine host, an old fat woman even now with
me; but she's gone.

units.speaker: SIMPLE
units.text:
Pray you, sir, was't not the wise woman of
Brentford?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Ay, marry, was it, mussel-shell: what would you with
her?

units.speaker: SIMPLE
units.text:
My master, sir, Master Slender, sent to her, seeing
her go through the streets, to know, sir, whether
one Nym, sir, that beguiled him of a chain, had the
chain or no.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: I spake with the old woman about it.

units.speaker: SIMPLE
units.text: And what says she, I pray, sir?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Marry, she says that the very same man that
beguiled Master Slender of his chain cozened him of
it.

units.speaker: SIMPLE
FALSTAFF
units.text: What are they? let us know.

units.speaker: HOST
units.text: Ay, come; quick.

units.speaker: SIMPLE
units.text: I may not conceal them, sir.

units.speaker: HOST
units.text: Conceal them, or thou diest.

units.speaker: SIMPLE
units.text:
Why, sir, they were nothing but about Mistress Anne
Page; to know if it were my master's fortune to
have her or no.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: 'Tis, 'tis his fortune.

units.speaker: SIMPLE
units.text: What, sir?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: To have her, or no. Go; say the woman told me so.

units.speaker: SIMPLE
units.text: May I be bold to say so, sir?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Ay, sir; like who more bold.

units.speaker: SIMPLE
units.text:
I thank your worship: I shall make my master glad
with these tidings.
Exit

units.speaker: HOST
units.text:
Thou art clerkly, thou art clerkly, Sir John. Was
there a wise woman with thee?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Ay, that there was, mine host; one that hath taught
me more wit than ever I learned before in my life;
and I paid nothing for it neither, but was paid for
my learning.
Enter BARDOLPH

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text: Out, alas, sir! cozenage, mere cozenage!

units.speaker: HOST
units.text: Where be my horses? speak well of them, varletto.

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text:
Run away with the cozeners; for so soon as I came
beyond Eton, they threw me off from behind one of
them, in a slough of mire; and set spurs and away,
like three German devils, three Doctor Faustuses.

units.speaker: HOST
units.text:
They are gone but to meet the duke, villain: do not
say they be fled; Germans are honest men.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text: Where is mine host?

units.speaker: HOST
units.text: What is the matter, sir?

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text:
Have a care of your entertainments: there is a
friend of mine come to town tells me there is three
cozen-germans that has cozened all the hosts of
Readins, of Maidenhead, of Colebrook, of horses and
money. I tell you for good will, look you: you
are wise and full of gibes and vlouting-stocks, and
'tis not convenient you should be cozened. Fare you
well.
Exit
Enter DOCTOR CAIUS

units.speaker: DOCTOR CAIUS
units.text: Vere is mine host de Jarteer?

units.speaker: HOST
units.text:
Here, master doctor, in perplexity and doubtful
dilemma.

units.speaker: DOCTOR CAIUS
units.text:
I cannot tell vat is dat: but it is tell-a me dat
you make grand preparation for a duke de Jamany: by
my trot, dere is no duke dat the court is know to
come. I tell you for good vill: adieu.
Exit

units.speaker: HOST
units.text:
Hue and cry, villain, go! Assist me, knight. I am
undone! Fly, run, hue and cry, villain! I am undone!
Exeunt Host and BARDOLPH

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
I would all the world might be cozened; for I have
been cozened and beaten too. If it should come to
the ear of the court, how I have been transformed
and how my transformation hath been washed and
cudgelled, they would melt me out of my fat drop by
drop and liquor fishermen's boots with me; I warrant
they would whip me with their fine wits till I were
as crest-fallen as a dried pear. I never prospered
since I forswore myself at primero. Well, if my
wind were but long enough to say my prayers, I would
repent.
Enter MISTRESS QUICKLY

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Now, whence come you?

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: From the two parties, forsooth.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
The devil take one party and his dam the other! and
so they shall be both bestowed. I have suffered more
for their sakes, more than the villanous inconstancy
of man's disposition is able to bear.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
And have not they suffered? Yes, I warrant;
speciously one of them; Mistress Ford, good heart,
is beaten black and blue, that you cannot see a
white spot about her.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
What tellest thou me of black and blue? I was
beaten myself into all the colours of the rainbow;
and I was like to be apprehended for the witch of
Brentford: but that my admirable dexterity of wit,
my counterfeiting the action of an old woman,
delivered me, the knave constable had set me i' the
stocks, i' the common stocks, for a witch.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Sir, let me speak with you in your chamber: you
shall hear how things go; and, I warrant, to your
content. Here is a letter will say somewhat. Good
hearts, what ado here is to bring you together!
Sure, one of you does not serve heaven well, that
you are so crossed.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Come up into my chamber.
Exeunt
Enter FENTON and Host

units.speaker: HOST
units.text:
Master Fenton, talk not to me; my mind is heavy: I
will give over all.

units.speaker: FENTON
units.text:
Yet hear me speak. Assist me in my purpose,
And, as I am a gentleman, I'll give thee
A hundred pound in gold more than your loss.

units.speaker: HOST
units.text:
I will hear you, Master Fenton; and I will at the
least keep your counsel.

units.speaker: FENTON
units.text:
From time to time I have acquainted you
With the dear love I bear to fair Anne Page;
Who mutually hath answer'd my affection,
So far forth as herself might be her chooser,
Even to my wish: I have a letter from her
Of such contents as you will wonder at;
The mirth whereof so larded with my matter,
That neither singly can be manifested,
Without the show of both; fat Falstaff
Hath a great scene: the image of the jest
I'll show you here at large. Hark, good mine host.
To-night at Herne's oak, just 'twixt twelve and one,
Must my sweet Nan present the Fairy Queen;
The purpose why, is here: in which disguise,
While other jests are something rank on foot,
Her father hath commanded her to slip
Away with Slender and with him at Eton
Immediately to marry: she hath consented: Now, sir,
Her mother, ever strong against that match
And firm for Doctor Caius, hath appointed
That he shall likewise shuffle her away,
While other sports are tasking of their minds,
And at the deanery, where a priest attends,
Straight marry her: to this her mother's plot
She seemingly obedient likewise hath
Made promise to the doctor. Now, thus it rests:
Her father means she shall be all in white,
And in that habit, when Slender sees his time
To take her by the hand and bid her go,
She shall go with him: her mother hath intended,
The better to denote her to the doctor,
For they must all be mask'd and vizarded,
That quaint in green she shall be loose enrobed,
With ribands pendent, flaring 'bout her head;
And when the doctor spies his vantage ripe,
To pinch her by the hand, and, on that token,
The maid hath given consent to go with him.

units.speaker: HOST
units.text: Which means she to deceive, father or mother?

units.speaker: FENTON
units.text:
Both, my good host, to go along with me:
And here it rests, that you'll procure the vicar
To stay for me at church 'twixt twelve and one,
And, in the lawful name of marrying,
To give our hearts united ceremony.

units.speaker: HOST
units.text:
Well, husband your device; I'll to the vicar:
Bring you the maid, you shall not lack a priest.

units.speaker: FENTON
units.text:
So shall I evermore be bound to thee;
Besides, I'll make a present recompense.
Exeunt
Enter FALSTAFF and MISTRESS QUICKLY

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Prithee, no more prattling; go. I'll hold. This is
the third time; I hope good luck lies in odd
numbers. Away I go. They say there is divinity in
odd numbers, either in nativity, chance, or death.
Away!

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
I'll provide you a chain; and I'll do what I can to
get you a pair of horns.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Away, I say; time wears: hold up your head, and mince.
Exit MISTRESS QUICKLY
Enter FORD

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
How now, Master Brook! Master Brook, the matter
will be known to-night, or never. Be you in the
Park about midnight, at Herne's oak, and you shall
see wonders.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text:
Went you not to her yesterday, sir, as you told me
you had appointed?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
I went to her, Master Brook, as you see, like a poor
old man: but I came from her, Master Brook, like a
poor old woman. That same knave Ford, her husband,
hath the finest mad devil of jealousy in him,
Master Brook, that ever governed frenzy. I will tell
you: he beat me grievously, in the shape of a
woman; for in the shape of man, Master Brook, I fear
not Goliath with a weaver's beam; because I know
also life is a shuttle. I am in haste; go along
with me: I'll tell you all, Master Brook. Since I
plucked geese, played truant and whipped top, I knew
not what 'twas to be beaten till lately. Follow
me: I'll tell you strange things of this knave
Ford, on whom to-night I will be revenged, and I
will deliver his wife into your hand. Follow.
Strange things in hand, Master Brook! Follow.
Exeunt
Enter PAGE, SHALLOW, and SLENDER

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
Come, come; we'll couch i' the castle-ditch till we
see the light of our fairies. Remember, son Slender,
my daughter.

units.speaker: SLENDER
units.text:
Ay, forsooth; I have spoke with her and we have a
nay-word how to know one another: I come to her in
white, and cry 'mum;' she cries 'budget;' and by
that we know one another.

units.speaker: SHALLOW
units.text:
That's good too: but what needs either your 'mum'
or her 'budget?' the white will decipher her well
enough. It hath struck ten o'clock.

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
The night is dark; light and spirits will become it
well. Heaven prosper our sport! No man means evil
but the devil, and we shall know him by his horns.
Let's away; follow me.
Exeunt
Enter MISTRESS PAGE, MISTRESS FORD, and DOCTOR CAIUS

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Master doctor, my daughter is in green: when you
see your time, take her by the band, away with her
to the deanery, and dispatch it quickly. Go before
into the Park: we two must go together.

units.speaker: DOCTOR CAIUS
units.text: I know vat I have to do. Adieu.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text: Fare you well, sir.
Exit DOCTOR CAIUS

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
My husband will not rejoice so much at the abuse of
Falstaff as he will chafe at the doctor's marrying
my daughter: but 'tis no matter; better a little
chiding than a great deal of heart-break.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
Where is Nan now and her troop of fairies, and the
Welsh devil Hugh?

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
They are all couched in a pit hard by Herne's oak,
with obscured lights; which, at the very instant of
Falstaff's and our meeting, they will at once
display to the night.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: That cannot choose but amaze him.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
If he be not amazed, he will be mocked; if he be
amazed, he will every way be mocked.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: We'll betray him finely.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Against such lewdsters and their lechery
Those that betray them do no treachery.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: The hour draws on. To the oak, to the oak!
Exeunt
Enter SIR HUGH EVANS, disguised, with others as Fairies

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text:
Trib, trib, fairies; come; and remember your parts:
be pold, I pray you; follow me into the pit; and
when I give the watch-'ords, do as I pid you:
come, come; trib, trib.
Exeunt
Enter FALSTAFF disguised as Herne

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
The Windsor bell hath struck twelve; the minute
draws on. Now, the hot-blooded gods assist me!
Remember, Jove, thou wast a bull for thy Europa; love
set on thy horns. O powerful love! that, in some
respects, makes a beast a man, in some other, a man
a beast. You were also, Jupiter, a swan for the love
of Leda. O omnipotent Love! how near the god drew
to the complexion of a goose! A fault done first in
the form of a beast. O Jove, a beastly fault! And
then another fault in the semblance of a fowl; think
on 't, Jove; a foul fault! When gods have hot
backs, what shall poor men do? For me, I am here a
Windsor stag; and the fattest, I think, i' the
forest. Send me a cool rut-time, Jove, or who can
blame me to piss my tallow? Who comes here? my
doe?
Enter MISTRESS FORD and MISTRESS PAGE

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: Sir John! art thou there, my deer? my male deer?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
My doe with the black scut! Let the sky rain
potatoes; let it thunder to the tune of Green
Sleeves, hail kissing-comfits and snow eringoes; let
there come a tempest of provocation, I will shelter me
here.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: Mistress Page is come with me, sweetheart.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Divide me like a bribe buck, each a haunch: I will
keep my sides to myself, my shoulders for the fellow
of this walk, and my horns I bequeath your husbands.
Am I a woodman, ha? Speak I like Herne the hunter?
Why, now is Cupid a child of conscience; he makes
restitution. As I am a true spirit, welcome!
Noise within

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text: Alas, what noise?

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text: Heaven forgive our sins

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: What should this be?

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text: Away, away!
They run off

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
I think the devil will not have me damned, lest the
oil that's in me should set hell on fire; he would
never else cross me thus.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Fairies, black, grey, green, and white,
You moonshine revellers and shades of night,
You orphan heirs of fixed destiny,
Attend your office and your quality.
Crier Hobgoblin, make the fairy oyes.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text:
Elves, list your names; silence, you airy toys.
Cricket, to Windsor chimneys shalt thou leap:
Where fires thou find'st unraked and hearths unswept,
There pinch the maids as blue as bilberry:
Our radiant queen hates sluts and sluttery.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
They are fairies; he that speaks to them shall die:
I'll wink and couch: no man their works must eye.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text:
Where's Bede? Go you, and where you find a maid
That, ere she sleep, has thrice her prayers said,
Raise up the organs of her fantasy;
Sleep she as sound as careless infancy:
But those as sleep and think not on their sins,
Pinch them, arms, legs, backs, shoulders, sides and
shins.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
About, about;
Search Windsor Castle, elves, within and out:
Strew good luck, ouphes, on every sacred room:
That it may stand till the perpetual doom,
In state as wholesome as in state 'tis fit,
Worthy the owner, and the owner it.
The several chairs of order look you scour
With juice of balm and every precious flower:
Each fair instalment, coat, and several crest,
With loyal blazon, evermore be blest!
And nightly, meadow-fairies, look you sing,
Like to the Garter's compass, in a ring:
The expressure that it bears, green let it be,
More fertile-fresh than all the field to see;
And 'Honi soit qui mal y pense' write
In emerald tufts, flowers purple, blue and white;
Let sapphire, pearl and rich embroidery,
Buckled below fair knighthood's bending knee:
Fairies use flowers for their charactery.
Away; disperse: but till 'tis one o'clock,
Our dance of custom round about the oak
Of Herne the hunter, let us not forget.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text:
Pray you, lock hand in hand; yourselves in order set
And twenty glow-worms shall our lanterns be,
To guide our measure round about the tree.
But, stay; I smell a man of middle-earth.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Heavens defend me from that Welsh fairy, lest he
transform me to a piece of cheese!

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text: Vile worm, thou wast o'erlook'd even in thy birth.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
With trial-fire touch me his finger-end:
If he be chaste, the flame will back descend
And turn him to no pain; but if he start,
It is the flesh of a corrupted heart.

units.speaker: PISTOL
units.text: A trial, come.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text: Come, will this wood take fire?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Oh, Oh, Oh!

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Corrupt, corrupt, and tainted in desire!
About him, fairies; sing a scornful rhyme;
And, as you trip, still pinch him to your time.
[SONG]
Fie on sinful fantasy!
Fie on lust and luxury!
Lust is but a bloody fire,
Kindled with unchaste desire,
Fed in heart, whose flames aspire
As thoughts do blow them, higher and higher.
Pinch him, fairies, mutually;
Pinch him for his villany;
Pinch him, and burn him, and turn him about,
Till candles and starlight and moonshine be out.

During this song they pinch FALSTAFF. DOCTOR CAIUS comes one way, and steals away a boy in green; SLENDER another way, and takes off a boy in white; and FENTON comes and steals away ANNE PAGE. A noise of hunting is heard within. All the Fairies run away. FALSTAFF pulls off his buck's head, and rises
Enter PAGE, FORD, MISTRESS PAGE, and MISTRESS FORD

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
Nay, do not fly; I think we have watch'd you now
Will none but Herne the hunter serve your turn?

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
I pray you, come, hold up the jest no higher
Now, good Sir John, how like you Windsor wives?
See you these, husband? do not these fair yokes
Become the forest better than the town?

units.speaker: FORD
units.text:
Now, sir, who's a cuckold now? Master Brook,
Falstaff's a knave, a cuckoldly knave; here are his
horns, Master Brook: and, Master Brook, he hath
enjoyed nothing of Ford's but his buck-basket, his
cudgel, and twenty pounds of money, which must be
paid to Master Brook; his horses are arrested for
it, Master Brook.

units.speaker: MISTRESS FORD
units.text:
Sir John, we have had ill luck; we could never meet.
I will never take you for my love again; but I will
always count you my deer.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: I do begin to perceive that I am made an ass.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: Ay, and an ox too: both the proofs are extant.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
And these are not fairies? I was three or four
times in the thought they were not fairies: and yet
the guiltiness of my mind, the sudden surprise of my
powers, drove the grossness of the foppery into a
received belief, in despite of the teeth of all
rhyme and reason, that they were fairies. See now
how wit may be made a Jack-a-Lent, when 'tis upon
ill employment!

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text:
Sir John Falstaff, serve Got, and leave your
desires, and fairies will not pinse you.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: Well said, fairy Hugh.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text: And leave your jealousies too, I pray you.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text:
I will never mistrust my wife again till thou art
able to woo her in good English.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Have I laid my brain in the sun and dried it, that
it wants matter to prevent so gross o'erreaching as
this? Am I ridden with a Welsh goat too? shall I
have a coxcomb of frize? 'Tis time I were choked
with a piece of toasted cheese.

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text:
Seese is not good to give putter; your belly is all
putter.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
'Seese' and 'putter'! have I lived to stand at the
taunt of one that makes fritters of English? This
is enough to be the decay of lust and late-walking
through the realm.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Why Sir John, do you think, though we would have the
virtue out of our hearts by the head and shoulders
and have given ourselves without scruple to hell,
that ever the devil could have made you our delight?

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: What, a hodge-pudding? a bag of flax?

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text: A puffed man?

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text: Old, cold, withered and of intolerable entrails?

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: And one that is as slanderous as Satan?

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text: And as poor as Job?

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: And as wicked as his wife?

units.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
units.text:
And given to fornications, and to taverns and sack
and wine and metheglins, and to drinkings and
swearings and starings, pribbles and prabbles?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Well, I am your theme: you have the start of me; I
am dejected; I am not able to answer the Welsh
flannel; ignorance itself is a plummet o'er me: use
me as you will.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text:
Marry, sir, we'll bring you to Windsor, to one
Master Brook, that you have cozened of money, to
whom you should have been a pander: over and above
that you have suffered, I think to repay that money
will be a biting affliction.

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
Yet be cheerful, knight: thou shalt eat a posset
to-night at my house; where I will desire thee to
laugh at my wife, that now laughs at thee: tell her
Master Slender hath married her daughter.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
[Aside] Doctors doubt that: if Anne Page be my
daughter, she is, by this, Doctor Caius' wife.
Enter SLENDER

units.speaker: SLENDER
units.text: Whoa ho! ho, father Page!

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text: Son, how now! how now, son! have you dispatched?

units.speaker: SLENDER
units.text:
Dispatched! I'll make the best in Gloucestershire
know on't; would I were hanged, la, else.

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text: Of what, son?

units.speaker: SLENDER
units.text:
I came yonder at Eton to marry Mistress Anne Page,
and she's a great lubberly boy. If it had not been
i' the church, I would have swinged him, or he
should have swinged me. If I did not think it had
been Anne Page, would I might never stir!—and 'tis
a postmaster's boy.

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text: Upon my life, then, you took the wrong.

units.speaker: SLENDER
units.text:
What need you tell me that? I think so, when I took
a boy for a girl. If I had been married to him, for
all he was in woman's apparel, I would not have had
him.

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
Why, this is your own folly. Did not I tell you how
you should know my daughter by her garments?

units.speaker: SLENDER
units.text:
I went to her in white, and cried 'mum,' and she
cried 'budget,' as Anne and I had appointed; and yet
it was not Anne, but a postmaster's boy.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE

units.text: Enter DOCTOR CAIUS

units.speaker: DOCTOR CAIUS
MISTRESS PAGE
units.text: Why, did you take her in green?

units.speaker: DOCTOR CAIUS
units.text:
Ay, by gar, and 'tis a boy: by gar, I'll raise all
Windsor.
Exit

units.speaker: FORD
units.text: This is strange. Who hath got the right Anne?

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text: My heart misgives me: here comes Master Fenton.
Enter FENTON and ANNE PAGE

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text: How now, Master Fenton!

units.speaker: ANNE PAGE
units.text: Pardon, good father! good my mother, pardon!

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
Now, mistress, how chance you went not with Master
Slender?

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text: Why went you not with master doctor, maid?

units.speaker: FENTON
units.text:
You do amaze her: hear the truth of it.
You would have married her most shamefully,
Where there was no proportion held in love.
The truth is, she and I, long since contracted,
Are now so sure that nothing can dissolve us.
The offence is holy that she hath committed;
And this deceit loses the name of craft,
Of disobedience, or unduteous title,
Since therein she doth evitate and shun
A thousand irreligious cursed hours,
Which forced marriage would have brought upon her.

units.speaker: FORD
FALSTAFF
units.text:
I am glad, though you have ta'en a special stand to
strike at me, that your arrow hath glanced.

units.speaker: PAGE
units.text:
Well, what remedy? Fenton, heaven give thee joy!
What cannot be eschew'd must be embraced.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: When night-dogs run, all sorts of deer are chased.

units.speaker: MISTRESS PAGE
units.text:
Well, I will muse no further. Master Fenton,
Heaven give you many, many merry days!
Good husband, let us every one go home,
And laugh this sport o'er by a country fire;
Sir John and all.

units.speaker: FORD
units.text:
Let it be so. Sir John,
To Master Brook you yet shall hold your word
For he tonight shall lie with Mistress Ford.
Exeunt

sentences.text: Enter SHALLOW, SLENDER, and SIR HUGH EVANS

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
Sir Hugh, persuade me not; I will make a Star-
chamber matter of it: if he were twenty Sir John
Falstaffs, he shall not abuse Robert Shallow, esquire.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
Sir Hugh, persuade me not; I will make a Star-
chamber matter of it: if he were twenty Sir John
Falstaffs, he shall not abuse Robert Shallow, esquire.

sentences.speaker: SLENDER
sentences.text:
In the county of Gloucester, justice of peace and
'Coram.'

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text: Ay, cousin Slender, and 'Custalourum.

sentences.speaker: SLENDER
sentences.text:
Ay, and 'Rato-lorum' too; and a gentleman born,
master parson; who writes himself 'Armigero,' in any
bill, warrant, quittance, or obligation, 'Armigero.'

sentences.speaker: SLENDER
sentences.text:
Ay, and 'Rato-lorum' too; and a gentleman born,
master parson; who writes himself 'Armigero,' in any
bill, warrant, quittance, or obligation, 'Armigero.'

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
Ay, that I do; and have done any time these three
hundred years.

sentences.speaker: SLENDER
sentences.text:
All his successors gone before him hath done't; and
all his ancestors that come after him may: they may
give the dozen white luces in their coat.

sentences.speaker: SLENDER
sentences.text:
All his successors gone before him hath done't; and
all his ancestors that come after him may: they may
give the dozen white luces in their coat.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text: It is an old coat.

sentences.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
SIR HUGH EVANS
SIR HUGH EVANS
SHALLOW
sentences.text:
The luce is the fresh fish; the salt fish is an old
coat.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
The luce is the fresh fish; the salt fish is an old
coat.

sentences.speaker: SLENDER
sentences.text: I may quarter, coz.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text: You may, by marrying.

sentences.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
sentences.text: It is marring indeed, if he quarter it.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text: Not a whit.

sentences.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
sentences.text:
Yes, py'r lady; if he has a quarter of your coat,
there is but three skirts for yourself, in my
simple conjectures: but that is all one. If Sir
John Falstaff have committed disparagements unto
you, I am of the church, and will be glad to do my
benevolence to make atonements and compromises
between you.

sentences.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
sentences.text:
Yes, py'r lady; if he has a quarter of your coat,
there is but three skirts for yourself, in my
simple conjectures: but that is all one. If Sir
John Falstaff have committed disparagements unto
you, I am of the church, and will be glad to do my
benevolence to make atonements and compromises
between you.

sentences.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
sentences.text:
Yes, py'r lady; if he has a quarter of your coat,
there is but three skirts for yourself, in my
simple conjectures: but that is all one. If Sir
John Falstaff have committed disparagements unto
you, I am of the church, and will be glad to do my

between you.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text: The council shall bear it; it is a riot.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text: The council shall bear it; it is a riot.

sentences.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
sentences.text:
It is not meet the council hear a riot; there is no
fear of Got in a riot: the council, look you, shall
desire to hear the fear of Got, and not to hear a
riot; take your vizaments in that.

sentences.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
sentences.text:
It is not meet the council hear a riot; there is no
fear of Got in a riot: the council, look you, shall
desire to hear the fear of Got, and not to hear a
riot; take your vizaments in that.

sentences.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS

fear of Got in a riot: the council, look you, shall
desire to hear the fear of Got, and not to hear a

sentences.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS

It is not meet the council hear a riot; there is no
fear of Got in a riot: the council, look you, shall

riot; take your vizaments in that.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
Ha! o' my life, if I were young again, the sword
should end it.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
Ha! o' my life, if I were young again, the sword
should end it.

sentences.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
sentences.text:
It is petter that friends is the sword, and end it:
and there is also another device in my prain, which
peradventure prings goot discretions with it: there
is Anne Page, which is daughter to Master Thomas
Page, which is pretty virginity.

sentences.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
sentences.text:
It is petter that friends is the sword, and end it:
and there is also another device in my prain, which
peradventure prings goot discretions with it: there
is Anne Page, which is daughter to Master Thomas
Page, which is pretty virginity.

sentences.speaker: SLENDER
sentences.text:
Mistress Anne Page? She has brown hair, and speaks
small like a woman.

sentences.speaker: SLENDER
sentences.text:
Mistress Anne Page? She has brown hair, and speaks
small like a woman.

sentences.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
sentences.text:
It is that fery person for all the orld, as just as
you will desire; and seven hundred pounds of moneys,
and gold and silver, is her grandsire upon his
death's-bed—Got deliver to a joyful resurrections!
—give, when she is able to overtake seventeen years
old: it were a goot motion if we leave our pribbles
and prabbles, and desire a marriage between Master
Abraham and Mistress Anne Page.

sentences.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
sentences.text:
It is that fery person for all the orld, as just as
you will desire; and seven hundred pounds of moneys,
and gold and silver, is her grandsire upon his

—give, when she is able to overtake seventeen years
old: it were a goot motion if we leave our pribbles

sentences.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: SLENDER
sentences.text: Did her grandsire leave her seven hundred pound?

sentences.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
sentences.text: Ay, and her father is make her a petter penny.

sentences.speaker: SLENDER
sentences.text: I know the young gentlewoman; she has good gifts.

sentences.speaker: SLENDER
sentences.text: I know the young gentlewoman; she has good gifts.

sentences.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
sentences.text: Seven hundred pounds and possibilities is goot gifts.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text: Well, let us see honest Master Page. Is Falstaff there?

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text: Well, let us see honest Master Page. Is Falstaff there?

sentences.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
sentences.text:
Shall I tell you a lie? I do despise a liar as I do
despise one that is false, or as I despise one that
is not true. The knight, Sir John, is there; and, I
beseech you, be ruled by your well-willers. I will
peat the door for Master Page.

sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
sentences.text: What, hoa! Got pless your house here!

sentences.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
sentences.text: What, hoa! Got pless your house here!

sentences.speaker: PAGE
sentences.text: [Within] Who's there?
Enter PAGE

sentences.speaker: SIR HUGH EVANS
sentences.text:
Here is Got's plessing, and your friend, and Justice
Shallow; and here young Master Slender, that
peradventures shall tell you another tale, if
matters grow to your likings.

sentences.speaker: PAGE
sentences.text:
I am glad to see your worships well.
I thank you for my venison, Master Shallow.

sentences.speaker: PAGE
sentences.text:
I am glad to see your worships well.
I thank you for my venison, Master Shallow.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
Master Page, I am glad to see you: much good do it
your good heart! I wished your venison better; it
was ill killed. How doth good Mistress Page?—and I
thank you always with my heart, la! with my heart.

sentences.speaker: SHALLOW
sentences.text:
Master Page, I am glad to see you: much good do it
your good heart! I wished your venison better; it
was ill killed. How doth good Mistress Page?—and I
thank you always with my heart, la! with my heart.
